meta.titleTemplate: Shisha Charcoal Packaging — Export & White-Label Options
meta.descriptionTemplate: Three-layer export packaging for coconut shisha charcoal: inner plastic, retail inner box, master carton. Neutral or white-label. MOQ {{moqTons}} tons FOB.

heroSubtitle: Export packaging for coconut shisha charcoal is a three-layer system — a sealed inner plastic, a printed retail inner box, and a corrugated master carton — supplied neutral or printed with your brand, with {{unNumber}} dangerous-goods and origin markings on every export carton.

atAGlance.heading: Packaging at a glance
atAGlance.definition: Every container is packed to order from three layers — inner plastic, inner box, master box — in one of three standard configurations, neutral or white-label.
atAGlance.proofValueTemplate: Digital proof in {{proofTimeDays}} working days + physical sample
atAGlance.pricesNote: White-label / custom-print add-on prices are indicative and confirmed on inquiry. Neutral packaging carries no printing surcharge.

intro.p1: Export packaging for coconut shisha charcoal is the three-layer system that moves briquettes from the factory floor to a retail shelf: a heat-sealed inner plastic that protects the charcoal from moisture, a printed inner box that presents it to the end customer, and a corrugated master box that carries it through ocean freight.
intro.p2: You choose two things independently: the configuration (which layers your order uses) and the branding (neutral or white-label). A neutral full configuration and a fully branded plastic-only order are both standard production — branding never restricts which layers you can combine.
intro.p3: Compliance is not optional in either case: every export master box carries {{unNumber}} dangerous-goods markings, country of origin, and lot coding, whether the carton is plain kraft or printed with your brand.

masterBox.definition: A master box — also called a master carton — is the corrugated outer shipping carton that holds packed inner boxes or bulk briquettes through container freight, stacking, and warehouse handling.
facts: Plain kraft or printed with your brand
Carries {{unNumber}} DG, origin, and net-weight markings

innerBox.definition: An inner box is the printed retail box the end customer sees — it holds plastic-wrapped charcoal, carries your brand and the retail markings, and stacks into the master carton.
facts: Matte or gloss coating as standard

plastic.definition: The inner plastic is the primary packaging — the first sealed layer around the charcoal and its moisture and dust barrier over four to eight weeks of sea transit.
facts: Clear or printed with your logo and lot code
The moisture barrier of the system — the box is presentation, the plastic is protection

additional.definition: Packaging add-ons are the optional layers beyond the plastic–box–carton stack: branded consumables, load securing, and transit protection.
facts: Anti-counterfeit set: QR, hologram, and 3D domed stickers
Branded tape, silica gel, box labels, and inserts from your artwork
Strapping, ISPM-15 palletization, thermal container lining

whiteLabel.definition: White-label production — also called private label or OEM — lets you sell our coconut shisha charcoal under your own brand: you supply the artwork, we provide the die-line and print it across every layer.
facts: Artwork in {{acceptedArtworkFormats}}; die-line and template provided
Digital proof in {{proofTimeDays}} working days, then a physical sample
Your design stays exclusive — NDA available
A single branded container is fine; no recurring commitment

configurations.intro: Three standard configurations cover the common buying scenarios. Mixed orders are normal — for example, part of a container in the full retail configuration and part in plastic-only bulk cartons.
configurations.tableCaption: Standard packaging configurations. Branding (neutral or white-label) is independent of configuration.

full.label: Full
full.bestFor: Retail shelves and distributors — shelf-ready in the destination market

standard.label: Standard
standard.bestFor: Buyers who repack into their own master cartons or floor displays

minimal.label: Minimal
minimal.bestFor: Cafés, lounges, and bulk buyers who don't need a retail box

branding.intro: Branding is a choice you make per order, independent of configuration. Both paths ship with full export compliance markings.

neutral.body: Neutral packaging — also called unbranded, plain, or generic — is plain kraft master cartons, generic inner boxes, and clear plastic. There is no printing surcharge and no extra minimum beyond the standard {{moqLabel}} order MOQ. Neutral is not blank: every export carton still carries the mandatory {{unNumber}} dangerous-goods, country-of-origin, and net-weight markings. It is the fastest, lowest-cost path to a loaded container.

whiteLabel.body: With white-label, we print your artwork across the inner plastic, inner box, and master carton. You receive a die-line and template, sign an NDA if you want one, and your design stays exclusive to you.

timeline.intro: Why neutral ships faster: a branded first order adds proofing and print setup ahead of production.

proofSample.body: Every branded order is approved twice before mass printing: a digital proof in {{proofTimeDays}} working days, then a physical pre-production sample of your packaging. Request both through the inquiry form or the samples page.

mandatory.h3: Mandatory on every export (neutral or branded)
mandatory.body1: Coconut charcoal ships as {{imdgLabel}} cargo. Carriers require the {{unNumber}} marking and Class {{unClass}} dangerous-goods labels on every master carton before they accept the booking — plain kraft and fully branded cartons carry exactly the same markings.
mandatory.body2: Alongside the DG set, every carton is printed with country of origin (Made in Indonesia), net weight, and a batch/lot code for traceability. Wood pallets, when palletization is requested, are ISPM-15 heat-treated and stamped.

retail.body: Retail inner boxes carry your barcode/EAN — buyer-supplied, factory-printed — plus net weight and origin. Shelf-ready formats are quoted per order.

items.q: What packaging options do you offer for shisha charcoal?
items.a: Three layers — sealed inner plastic ({{innerPlasticSizes}}), printed inner box ({{innerBoxSizes}}, {{innerBoxGsm}} gsm), and a {{masterBoxWallOptions}}-wall master carton ({{masterBoxSizes}} net) — combined in three standard configurations: Full (all three layers), Standard (plastic + inner box), and Minimal (plastic + master box). Each ships neutral or white-label.

items.q: Can you produce shisha charcoal under my own brand (white label / OEM)?
items.a: Yes. White-label (private label / OEM) production is standard: you supply print-ready artwork ({{acceptedArtworkFormats}}, CMYK), we provide the die-line and template, print your brand across the plastic, inner box, and master carton, and ship under your name. Your design stays exclusive to you and an NDA is available.

items.q: Do you offer neutral or unbranded packaging?
items.a: Yes. Neutral packaging — plain kraft master cartons, generic inner boxes, clear plastic — carries no printing surcharge and no minimum beyond the standard {{moqLabel}} order MOQ. Note that neutral is not blank: {{unNumber}} dangerous-goods markings, country of origin, and net weight are still printed on every export carton, as carriers require.

items.q: How much does custom-printed packaging cost?
items.a: Custom-print costs are add-ons on top of the charcoal FOB price — inner-box printing is priced per kg, and finish upgrades (lamination, emboss, UV spot, foil) and a double-wall master-box upgrade are priced per ton. Current rates are quoted on inquiry, {{portLabel}}. Neutral packaging carries no printing surcharge.

items.q: What is the minimum order for custom-printed packaging?
items.a: The custom-print minimum counts per printed design, on top of the standard order MOQ of {{moqLabel}}. A single branded container is fine — there is no recurring-order commitment, and you can run multiple designs or sizes in one container. The exact per-design minimum is confirmed on inquiry.

items.q: Do you send a digital proof and physical sample before printing?
items.a: Yes, both. You receive a digital proof within {{proofTimeDays}} working days of final artwork, and a physical pre-production sample of your packaging before the production run. Nothing is mass-printed until you approve both.

items.q: How long does a custom packaging order take?
items.a: A first branded order runs about {{newBrandLeadTime}} days from approved artwork: digital proof in {{proofTimeDays}} working days, physical sample, then print and production. Repeat orders with artwork on file run about {{repeatBrandLeadTime}} days. Neutral orders skip proofing entirely and follow the standard {{leadTimeLabel}} production window.

items.q: How many master boxes fit in a 20ft container?
items.a: A 20ft container loads {{containerNetKg}} kg of charcoal net. Loose-loaded, that is {{boxesPerContainerExample}} master boxes. Palletized counts are lower and quoted per order, since pallet size and box mix change the math.

items.q: Do you provide a die-line or packaging template?
items.a: Yes. We supply the die-line and template for every printed item — you place your artwork on it and return print-ready files ({{acceptedArtworkFormats}}). We print your design; we don't design it.

items.q: What finishes are available on the inner box?
items.a: Inner boxes ship on {{innerBoxGsm}} gsm board with a matte or gloss coating as standard. Upgrade finishes — lamination, emboss, UV spot, foil — are available per design and quoted on inquiry.

items.q: Can you print my retail barcode, country of origin, and net weight?
items.a: Yes. Retail inner boxes are printed with your barcode/EAN — you supply the number, we print it — plus net weight, country of origin, and batch/lot code. We don't issue EAN numbers; registration stays with your brand.

items.q: Are UN 1361 dangerous-goods markings printed on the packaging?
items.a: Yes, on every master carton without exception. Coconut charcoal ships as {{imdgLabel}} cargo, and carriers require the {{unNumber}} marking and Class {{unClass}} DG labels before accepting the booking — on neutral and branded cartons alike.

items.q: Is my packaging design kept confidential?
items.a: Yes. Your artwork and design are exclusive to you — never reused or sold to another buyer — and we sign an NDA on request. Product or territory exclusivity is a separate arrangement, available by negotiation.
